summary: Large mnmainrule decompilation pass for the main rules menu. The PR replaced several `/// #...` stubs with C, expanded the shared menu-rule user_data layout, typed function prototypes, and improved matching in `main/melee/mn/mnmainrule`; decomp.dev reported two new 100% matches and ten other improvements. The only grhomerun changes in the slice are formatting reflows, not evidence of logic changes.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation work; implemented rule-menu input handling, option animation/update helpers, menu GObj creation, and supporting data/extern declarations.
Expanded `struct mn_802307F8_t` layout and replaced several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with typed prototypes.
Formatting-only line wrapping/reflow in shown diff; no logic change evident.

what_changed: Implemented C bodies for previously stubbed mnmainrule functions including `fn_8022F538`, `mn_8022FB88`, `mn_8022FD18`, `mn_8022FEC8`, `mn_80230198`, `mn_80230274`, `fn_802309F0`, and `mn_80230E38`.
Added local helper layouts such as `struct mn_803EC818_t`, `struct mn_8022FB88_arg1_t`, and `struct mn_8022FEC8_jobj_ref_t`, plus animation-loop tables `mn_803EC734` and `mn_803EC770`.

Reworked `mn_802307F8` from a partial text-label helper into a fully matched typed implementation that frees/recreates `HSD_Text`, selects a label index from `mn_803EC818`/`mn_804D4B96`, and uses external f32 globals for SisLib placement/font constants.

Replaced the local `mn_80230D18_t` with the shared header struct `mn_802307F8_t`; `mn_80230D18` now initializes menu state from `mn_804A04F0` and `GameRules`, handles the `gm_801A4310() == 0x1B` time-limit special case, records text/user_data state, and populates JObj slots with `lb_80011E24`.

Added `mn_80230E38` logic to create and configure the main-rule menu GObj, load `MenMainConRl_Top`, add animations, allocate/free user_data through `HSD_MemAlloc`/`GObj_InitUserData`, build cursor/option JObjs, and initialize option visuals.
Added `fn_8022F538` and `fn_802309F0` logic for controller input, selected/confirmed option updates, menu transitions, audio feedback, GameRules writes, and per-frame animation state updates.

Expanded `struct mn_802307F8_t` in the header with explicit byte fields `x3`, `x4`, `x5`, `x6`, `x9`, a union overlay for flat `xC[17]` JObj slots and nested option roots `x34[7]`, and the `HSD_Text* text` at offset 0x130.
Typed prototypes for `mn_8022FB88`, `mn_8022FD18`, `mn_8022FEC8`, `mn_80230198`, `mn_80230274`, `fn_802309F0`, and `mn_80230D18` instead of leaving them as unknown signatures.
Adjusted `src/melee/gr/grhomerun.c` formatting around long casts, loops, and `HSD_JObjSetTranslateX` calls; the diff excerpt does not show semantic changes there.
smart_moves: Modeling `mn_803EC818` as a 5-byte-stride struct with an `x0` byte and padding let the code express both first-entry and indexed-byte accesses without pretending the data was a simple flat `u8[]`.

The header union for `mn_802307F8_t` keeps one canonical allocation layout while allowing both flat child-slot access (`xC[17]`) and per-option access (`x34[7]`), avoiding duplicate local structs for the same user_data.

The PR preserved decompiler-shaped control flow where needed for matching, including `PAD_STACK(8)`, explicit gotos in `mn_80230198`, repeated state-switch logic, and low-level pointer casts instead of over-cleaning the C.
Animation selection is centralized around `AnimLoopSettings` and `mn_803EC600` offset casts, which made rule-option visual state easier to express consistently across creation and update paths.
The code writes changed menu choices back into `GameRules` through `gmMainLib_8015CC34()` in both input/transition and update paths, matching the UI's role as the source of rule settings.

The implementation keeps special cases visible rather than hiding them: mode `0x1B` skips option 4 and changes time-limit behavior, `mn_804D6BD4` disables handicap value 1, and mode/time/stock handling differs when rule mode is 1.
decomp_lessons: When a user_data struct is discovered across multiple functions, move the real shared layout into the header and remove throwaway local structs; this PR replaced `mn_80230D18_t` with `mn_802307F8_t`.

For menu files, JObj trees often need dual representations: a raw child-slot array for `lb_80011E24`/`lb_8001204C`, and semantic-ish grouped fields for option-specific roots. A union overlay can preserve offsets while improving call-site clarity.

Do not force semantic names too early. The PR keeps offset-based fields such as `x0`, `x1`, `x34`, `x58`, `xA8`, and `pad_*` where the exact meaning is still weak, but still types pointers and sizes enough to unblock matching.

Packed byte-index data may need stack copies and byte-pointer iteration to reproduce the original load pattern; examples include loops over `u16`, `s32`, and pointer-sized temporaries used as JObj index lists.

External f32/data labels can be preferable to literal constants when the original code references SDA/data objects; `mn_802307F8` now uses `mn_804DBE18` through `mn_804DBE2C` for text creation and font size.

A bot-reported full match for a small helper can still require broad surrounding structure work: `mn_802307F8` only gained +35 matched bytes, but the diff also clarified the header layout and data access pattern used by larger functions.

Leave known nonmatching larger bodies clearly decompiler-shaped until all data and control-flow details are understood; decomp.dev showed many large improvements but not full matches for functions such as `mn_80230E38`, `fn_8022F538`, and `mn_80230274`.
naming_conventions: Menu functions retain address-based `mn_802xxxxx` names until semantics are known; unnamed callbacks/procs use the `fn_802xxxxx` convention.
Helper structs are named by owning symbol/address and role, e.g. `mn_8022FB88_arg1_t`, `mn_8022FEC8_jobj_ref_t`, and `mn_803EC818_t`.
Unknown struct fields use offset-style `xN` names and explicit `pad_*` arrays, preserving layout evidence without inventing meanings.
Asset globals use existing descriptive resource names such as `MenMainConRl_Top`, `MenMainCursorRl_Top`, `MenMainNmRl_Top`, `MenMainCursorIs_Top`, and `MenMainConSs_Top`.
Data symbols remain address-based (`mn_803EC600`, `mn_803EC734`, `mn_803EC770`, `mn_804DBE18`, etc.) when no semantic name is evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `mn_80230198` uses `PAD_STACK(8)` and explicit branch labels/gotos (`seven`, `func`) to match the original control flow instead of idiomatic C.
Several routines cast `mn_803EC600` plus hard-coded offsets to `f32*`, `AnimLoopSettings*`, or `StaticModelDesc**`, preserving the original table layout and relocation behavior.
The code iterates over byte views of stack temporaries such as `sp14`, `spC`, `bytes4`, and `bytes2` to feed JObj index lists, a useful tactic when the source data is packed or awkwardly typed.
`mn_80230274` and `mn_80230E38` repeatedly use `lb_8001204C(..., roots, indices, 0x11)` to recover JObj child roots and then drive exact animation/frame calls on specific root indices.
`fn_8022F538` writes controller state through `((u32*) &mn_804A04F0.buttons)[...]`, preserving the apparent memory layout of the global menu-state object.

The PR retains repeated checks and switches, such as multiple `gm_801A4310()` branches and duplicated animation-state switch blocks in `fn_802309F0`, which is often necessary before a larger function fully matches.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews are present in the slice; intent is inferred from the diff and decomp.dev report.
decomp.dev GALE01 report: overall matched code increased to 66.33% (+0.01%, +468 bytes).
decomp.dev reported two new full matches in `main/melee/mn/mnmainrule`: `mn_80230198` (+220 bytes, 0% to 100%) and `mn_802307F8` (+35 bytes, 85.89% to 100%).

decomp.dev reported major unmatched-function improvements: `mn_80230E38` to 69.26%, `fn_8022F538` to 84.02%, `mn_80230274` to 71.56%, `fn_802309F0` to 93.99%, `mn_8022FEC8` to 81.66%, `mn_8022FB88` to 99.50%, and `mn_8022FD18` to 88.20%.
decomp.dev also reported a `.sdata2` regression of -10 bytes, from 50.00% to 36.36%, so future work should re-check constant/data placement around this file.
follow_up_queries: fn_8022F538 GameRules button handling gm_801A4310 duplicate branch
mn_80230274 option_roots xC x34 union JObj animation mismatch
